info.title: Azure Media Services Asset Migration Tool
info.description: A command line tool to migrate your media assets from Azure Media Services.

hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

commands.name: analyze
commands.description:
Analyze assets for migration and generate report.
Example(s):
mediamigrate analyze -s <subscriptionid> -g <resourcegroup> -n <account>
This will analyze the given media account and produce a summary report.

commands.name: assets
commands.description:
Migrate Assets
Examples:
mediamigrate assets -s <subscription id> -g <resource group> -n <account name> -a <storage account> -t path-template
This migrates the assets to a different storage account in your subscription.

commands.name: storage
commands.description:
Directly migrate the assets from the storage account.
Doesn't require the Azure media services to be running.
